howItWorks.intro: This gym membership cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Cost = Monthly × Months + Initiation Fee
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is the average gym membership cost?
faq.answer: Budget gyms: $10-30/month. Mid-range: $30-60/month. Premium: $60-200/month. CrossFit/boutique: $150-300/month. Most gyms charge a one-time initiation fee.

faq.question: How do I get the best deal?
faq.answer: Sign up in January (New Year sales), negotiate the initiation fee (it is almost always negotiable), ask for a free trial first, and check if your employer or insurance offers gym discounts.

faq.question: Is a gym membership worth it?
faq.answer: At $50/month and 15 visits, that is $3.33 per visit. Cheaper than most fitness classes ($15-30), personal training ($60-150/session), or boutique studios.

faq.question: What about hidden fees?
faq.answer: Watch for annual maintenance fees ($30-50), cancellation fees, personal training upsells, and automatic renewal. Read the full contract carefully.
